titulo_en: QR Codes Were Never Meant for Smartphones 📱🖥️⚡
descricao_en:
You probably scanned a QR code today without thinking twice.

But what if I told you it was never designed for restaurants, payments, or smartphones?

In 1994, a team of Japanese engineers faced a manufacturing problem that traditional barcodes simply couldn't solve. Their solution became one of the most important technologies of the modern world—used billions of times every day.

In this episode of Blueprints of History, discover the fascinating true story behind the invention of the QR Code, why it was created, and how one factory innovation quietly transformed everyday life.

If you enjoy hidden histories, engineering breakthroughs, and the untold stories behind everyday technology, consider subscribing for more documentaries every week.

titulo_en: The history of QR codes you might not know!
descricao_en:
In 1994, a Japanese engineer named Masahiro Hara, working at a Toyota subsidiary called Denso Wave, invented a small black-and-white square that could hold a lot more information than a barcode. 

He called it a QR code.

Then his company did something unusual. They held the patent — but they decided not to enforce it. The QR code was effectively given away to the world.

For nearly two decades, almost nothing happened. Smartphones didn't exist yet. The QR code drifted quietly through Japanese factories, Toyota car brochures, even juice packs.

Then, around 2010, it started to enter India. The Midday newspaper was an early adopter. Newspaper ads followed. Juice cartons. Then, eventually, Paytm — and the entire payments revolution that grew on top of it.

None of that happens if Denso Wave had decided to license the QR code instead.

Episode 2 of Intermission tells this story in the larger context of how Bajaj Finance and the rest of the Indian payments infrastructure got built.

titulo_en: The densest place on Earth: Hong Kong's "Sunless City"! 🏙️🚫
descricao_en:
Could you live in a place where the police didn't come in and the sun never hit the ground? 😱

Discover Kowloon Walled City, Hong Kong's former slum that became the most densely populated place in history. With more than 33,000 people crammed into just two blocks of buildings, it was a labyrinth of dark alleys, sweatshops and self-management.

In this video, we show what life was like inside this unique ecosystem before its demolition in 1993.

titulo_en: Who Invented QR Codes? | The Story Behind the Scan #originstories #facts #history
descricao_en:
You scan QR codes every day — for payments, menus, tickets.

But who created them?

The QR code was invented in 1994 by Masahiro Hara, an engineer at Denso Wave.

It was originally designed to track car parts in manufacturing — faster and more efficiently than barcodes.

Unlike traditional barcodes, QR codes can store much more data and can be scanned from any angle.

And here’s the surprising part:
The company made QR codes free to use globally — which is why they spread everywhere.

From factories…
to your phone camera.

at first i thought it was completely okay after all she was sick but eventually lena got older and healthier she no longer needed any devices and was able to live a normal life but she had learned something being sick brings attention and attention brings everything suddenly she started getting headaches whenever she had to do homework my parents immediately took them away from her and replaced them with ice cream or television when lena was 11 and i was 16 she had perfected her acting and worst of all her attacks always happened right when it came to me when i made the school team stomach pain when i got accepted to study collapsed at dinner emergency room nothing found and every time she secretly grinned at me as soon as our parents looked away she even practiced her pain faces in chats in which i allegedly insulted her and even wished her death he broke up with me in a coffee shop showed me the evidence on his phone and said he couldn t be with someone who was so cruel to his made a plan last sunday the time had come family dinner everyone there 20 minutes before i went into the bathroom and recorded a video i calmly explained that i was about to announce a fake promotion titulo_en: KRATOS: The Story of Gaming's Deepest Trauma PT 04 #goodofwar #kratos #kratosgodofwar #gow
descricao_en:
Forget about killing and epic fights. Kratos' real battle was never against Zeus, Odin, or Ares. The Ghost of Sparta's greatest war was fought within his own mind.
​In today's BioLendas video, we delve into the depths of the God of War's psychology. What happens to the mind of a man who carries the ashes of his murdered wife and daughter stuck to his own skin? Discover how irrational rage in Ancient Greece was just a mechanism to numb trauma, why Kratos hid the Blades of Chaos and tried to repress his feelings in the Norse era, and how he finally broke the cycle of violence to save the mind of his son, Atreus.
​From absolute trauma to self-forgiveness. Understand why Kratos' story transcends the world of games and is one of the greatest works about redemption, fatherhood and emotional survival in pop culture. 🪓❄️

opened the door in just his boxers his eyes strangely staring sandra is busy he says i saw her lying unconscious on the sofa at lunchtime empty bottles everywhere the children sat in their rooms hungry trying to do homework in the fourth month it got even worse the children smelled of smoke and something chemical mia had breathing problems the school nurse warned me i bought them new clothes you dare mention my father she screamed i was shocked that wasn t my friend anymore please sandra go to therapy i ll take care of the children she grew cold you always wanted my life now you want to take my children away from me my heart broke behind her was lukas holding ben it s the drugs i said quietly think of me of ben she pushed me towards the door never come here again or i ll call the police the door slammed shut i saw ben crying then i knew what i had to do the next day the children waited in vain for their mother again i picked them up i have to tell you something i said with t do anything wrong where are we going asked lukas i will do everything so that you can stay with me do you promise that asked mia with tears in her eyes i promise when we turned onto her street the his sleep just like i told him to our neighbor mr krüger is suing us for what his lawyer constantly describes as intentional genocide and is seriously demanding a severe punishment for my kindergarten child yes exactly for a five year old this completely absurd situation began three weeks ago when i taught tim to ride a tricycle without training wheels tim really wanted to learn this before his sixth birthday because his older cousin jonas could already ride a real bike and tim definitely didn t want to be outdone by anyone we practiced for about an hour that day tim wobbled along the sidewalk tongue sticking out in concentration his superhero helmet slightly askew on his head eventually he let go and he managed it he actually drove all alone for about ten glorious seconds he turned to me grinned broadly and shouted dad i can do it just then he veered off the sidewalk overcorrected and careened straight into krüger s front yard the tricycle crashed into a decorative stone tim flew through the air as if in slow motion and landed with a good thud in the middle of krüger s lawn mr krüger stormed out of his house as if someone had his car lit and screamed his poor babies had been murdered while i was checking to see if tim was okay he was crying more from shock than from pain krüger was already kneeling in the garden and examining his plants like a crime scene investigator he took photos from every angle measured the dent in the grass set up small evidence markers apparently he actually had it ready then he got out a tape measure and documented exactly where tim had landed less than an hour later he called the police the officers came by and visibly had to hold themselves together not to laugh when krüger demanded that they arrest tim for mass murder when they politely declined and said it was more of a civil matter krüger called his brother in law attorney dr sea and before i knew it my little son was the accused the lawsuit said tim committed an act of terrorism ange demanded 50 000 in damages plus criminal prosecution including the death penalty during the hearing dr see your evidence 37 high resolution photos of the crime scene from all possible perspectives mr krüger took the stand and actually began to cry real tears he said tim had wiped out innocent lives his babies that he had grown himself from seeds he spoke of emotional trauma of not being able to sleep at night because the perpetrator next door was freely riding a tricycle his wife giesela nodded dramatically and fanned her face catching her breath and holding a tissue at the ready as if she was about to pass out dr meer repeatedly showed the photos called it a massacre and spoke of callous disregard for life and sociopathic tendencies and i couldn t believe this was really happening should my son seriously go to prison when it was our turn our young public defender ms schneider stood up and asked to show the photos on the screen again she slowly clicked through the pictures zooming in on photo then 13 then 14 mr krüger she said calmly but firmly can you please show exactly which plants were destroyed krüger jumped up and pointed dramatically at the screen there my babies all dead ms schneider zoomed in further this is grass just normal lawn then she showed the next picture the impact site completely surrounded by completely intact daffodils tulips and roses your actual garden plants all remained completely undamaged this child she said turning to the jury did samhau manage to land precisely without touching a single real plant only on her lawn krueger s face turned bright red but the lawn the lawn is ruined he cried desperately the judge couldn t hold on any longer he tried to suppress his laughter but it turned into a strange snort that lasted throughout the entire room the bailiff bit his fist the note taker had long since stopped typing even krüger s wife had to suppress a smile the judge took a moment to compose himself and then said you are seeking the death penalty for a five year old child for falling on your lawn kruger nodded vigorously my lawn deserves justice in your honor the judge banged the gavel once looked at tim who had just woken up and asked if we should go get some ice cream now and said case dismissed mr krüger i recommend you put up a sign do not enter the lawn

was that he forgot about our anniversary and then acted like the gas station flowers he quickly bought on the way were planned he gave it to me still in the plastic packaging with a price tag my second friend s surprise was a trip to las vegas i was really excited until i realized it was actually for his buddy s bachelorette party i held bags for three days while they got drunk in clubs my third friend s surprise was the worst he broke up with me at my own birthday dinner right after the waiter brought the cake he said he didn t want to ruin my birthday by doing it beforehand so he waited until dessert as if that would somehow have been better so when leon put a blindfold on my pillow and told me to take saturday off all alarm bells went off in my head we had been together for two years good years really good years but i ve had good years before good years mean nothing when the bad day comes just trust me he said and led me to his car i honestly wanted it but mine hands just wouldn t stop shaking the journey took about 40 minutes i tried to guess where we were based on the curves and sounds first the highway then smaller roads then gravel nothing made sense as we stopped and he helped me get out i heard birds lots of birds chirping screeching whistling it almost sounded like a jungle then he took off my blindfold we stood in front of a large bird aviary in the city i squinted at the sign and didn t understand it at first then it occurred to me i mentioned this place once maybe six months ago i had seen a flyer in a coffee shop and said it looked cool just by the way while we were waiting for our ltees i didn t even think he was listening he was on his cell phone you remembered that i whispered leon smiled but you could tell he was nervous two years with you of course i m listening none of my ex boyfriends really listened to her not once i could have cried right there in the parking lot it was beautiful inside families were sitting everywhere children enthusiastically pointed to the birdcages along the walls budgies were chirping a colorful macaw croaked loudly somewhere a cockatoo puffed a little tune and said we sat down in the middle and i couldn t stop smiling but leon was totally he fidgeted with his hands constantly looking at his cell phone adjusting his collar bounced her leg and suddenly she was there again this fear what if this was all just preparation for bad news what if he took me somewhere nice so i wouldn t make a fuss when he broke up my third friend did exactly that a fancy restaurant so i m too embarrassed to cry what if leon has the same plan i started picking at my nails nervously like i used to do as a child then a woman in khakis came onto the stage with a gray dove in her arms good afternoon everyone this is bruno he is a carrier pigeon give him a banknote and he will bring you back a special message in return the most expensive fortune cookie in the world so to speak people laughed a little girl went first bruno flew gently to her took her bill and came back her message read aenteuer is waiting she was beaming from ear to ear an older man tried it afterwards his message was wisdom grows with age he grinned and showed it to his wife leon nudged me you should do this too i don t know come on to me he still seemed nervous but something in his voice made me reach into my pocket i found a 5 i notice the little things because nothing about you is small for me do you want to marry me i ll leave it three times tears blurred the words when i looked up leon was no longer in his seat he knelt gancho_primeira_frase: which spontaneous decision changed your life i was standing at my bus stop when i
texto_falado_limpo_en: which spontaneous decision changed your life i was standing at my bus stop when i noticed a beautiful woman waiting alone next to me the bus was late and at that very moment this woman got a call from a man who yelled at her insulted her and threatened her if she didn t come home immediately as soon as the man hung up i asked her if she was okay and she just stared at me i knew she wanted to tell me something but was afraid so i said you know i grew up that way too it took me a long time to understand how much i deserve love and how little i deserved what i had to go through when i said that she got tears in her eyes i told her that she didn t have to go to his house and that she could stay with me until she had enough money to move somewhere however i didn t get any answer from her i wrote down my number and told her to call me if she ever needed it that same night she called me completely hysterical crying and begging me to come and pick her up i got in my car and drove to the location she sent me when she got into the car she was injured and her face was covered in blood i spent the night with her in the hospital her name was poppy and soon she moved in with me it was supposed to be temporary but she never moved out again it s been 7 years now we have a daughter together and she has never been in a more loving and supportive environment if my bus hadn t been late back then who knows where this wonderful woman would be today

gancho_primeira_frase: i kept seeing my birth mother but she never knew it was me and at
texto_falado_limpo_en: i kept seeing my birth mother but she never knew it was me and at some point i did something i could never undo my mother had me when she was only 14 as far as i know she lived in very difficult circumstances at the time and had no opportunity to raise me that s why i was given up for adoption at the age of six i began to wonder who my mother was and my foster parents told me about her she sounded incredible in her stories i was really fascinated by her finally on my twelfth birthday they gave me the letter she had written for me he was full of tear stains in it she wrote that she couldn t be the mother i deserved but also that she was open to contact so i spent my entire youth looking for her my grades got worse i barely had any friends because i spent every free minute trying to find them and then at 22 i finally found her i couldn t believe it she worked at a small restaurant in the same state as me as soon as i found it i was already in the car on the way there i will never forget the first time i walked into this restaurant i immediately recognized her behind the counter and had to run straight to the toilet to cry uncontrollably after a few minutes i went back out and sat down and she of all people came to mine to take order i would like some coffee were the first words i ever said to my mother i wanted to tell her right away but i couldn t every time i tried i lost my voice and my heart raced so i left again that day after eating pancakes i didn t want and drinking coffee i didn t want either still going there became a habit even though the restaurant was over two hours away i drove there three times a week every time i talked to her and not once did she recognize me but every conversation healed a part of my inner child she was incredibly warm and genuine but nothing she said even suggested that she knew who i was after about 5 months i decided i finally had to work up the courage i even went as far as promising my best friend 500 if i didn t do it just to get myself to do it i went there again that day there were still three hours until closing time and i waited in the car the whole time i didn t get out once only when she finally got off work and came outside did i approach her she smiled and said kindly that they were already closed but i told her that that wasn t why i was here that there was something important that she needed to know i saw confusion come over her face especially as i apologized for keeping it a secret from her for so long she still didn t understand so i pulled out the letter my foster parents had given me i was about to open it when she suddenly started crying the next moment she threw her arms around my neck and hugged me so tightly that her tears soaked my jacket then she pulled away briefly and carefully asked if she could hug me at all but instead of answering i just pulled her close again we just stood there in the parking lot hugging and crying and then for the first time i said the words that i am her daughter it hit her deeply so hard that her legs gave out and i had to hold her and then she said something that completely overwhelmed me just look how big you ve become after we had composed ourselves she went back into the restaurant opened the door and we sat down together drank coffee ate a piece of cake and talked and during that conversation she said something that i will never forget she told me that it was no coincidence that she lived in the same state as me in truth she always wanted to be my mother so much so that she never moved away just because she knew there was a chance that i would look for her one day she had turned down job offers and even refrained from moving abroad with previous partners all in the hope that i would might find it easier one day when she told me that i cried so hard we ended up exchanging our numbers and after i left she wrote me a message thanking me for giving her this gift a gift she never knew would come this saturday she s coming to dinner with me to meet my girlfriend and i can t wait

gancho_primeira_frase: when did you realize that your best friend never actually liked you i was always
texto_falado_limpo_en: when did you realize that your best friend never actually liked you i was always the stupid ugly fat friend and honestly i wouldn t have it any other way at school my best friend lena not only got all the boys she wanted but also the attention of everyone else she was simply beautiful perfect blonde hair always freshly styled she smelled like expensive perfume and had the figure everyone dreamed of when lena and i went to the library together and guys only talked to me to get to her i was still happy for her and if her snapchat was full of messages from attractive guys while i only had messages from team snapchat and my mom then i was still happy for her not because she didn t constantly rub it in my face she did and not because she didn t think she was better than me she definitely did but because if i had to choose which one of us was the ugly girlfriend i would have chosen myself every time because even though lena was a bit superficial at times i knew deep down that she had a good heart and i thought that she deserved the world to be kind to her more than i did there was only one thing that made me sad i knew she would rely far too much on her looks to get through life and after graduating from school my guess confirmed pretty quickly i finished with an a while lena barely got through and that was only because an unpleasant tutor had his eye on her and ensured that her grades were viewed favorably well i was surprised when she worked all summer because you would have thought that she never had to work but when i visited her at her job i realized that working meant something completely different to her than it did to me she worked in a hip boutique downtown the boss took one look at her and immediately said she would be perfect for the store s image i on the other hand sat in a small tax office all summer and typed numbers into the computer monotonous work where no one would have called me perfect the more we talked to each other the more it became clear to me that we now saw the world completely differently at some point i even had to distance myself from her instead of calling or texting each other i only kept up with what was happening in their lives via instagram within 6 months her account grew from a few thousand to hundreds of thousands of followers her comments were full of men chasing after her she got free clothes was invited to events flew to festivals but i knew that a lot of these people were just using her that s why i always wrote lovely comments under her posts and sent her little packages of things she liked for her birthday i wanted her to know there was someone me who liked her for more than just her looks or her reach i honestly thought i would never see her again until i was 28 i was now a lawyer had my own apartment and a boyfriend who loved me for who i was maybe all these milestones reminded me of the past because one day i just wrote to lena again i wanted to know if she had changed if maybe we could be friends again we met at the ice cream parlor where we used to spend our pocket money when i saw her i had to suppress my reaction her face was full of botox her lips were filled and a thick layer of make up after that awkward hey how are you i got straight to the point i asked her if she was currently working she pushed around i just smiled because i had brought her a job offer i told her i was looking for a secretary 65 000 a year with good additional benefits she looked at me and said do i look like a welfare case to you then she started laughing like it was the funniest thing ever almost 10 years had passed and she was still the same i tried to stay calm and keep talking but she interrupted me do you actually know that i m an influencer she showed me her profile hardly any new followers news from years ago then i realized she had never been a misunderstood pretty girl she was just someone who peaked in high school and then stopped i wanted to leave but she stopped me oh don t be sad if i had such a flat butt i would have become a lawyer i just wanted to get away but she stood in my way and when will i meet your boyfriend or are you afraid that he will like me better i looked at her lena calm down you ve built your entire personality around being the pretty one and now there s nothing left of that so do me a favor and just leave me alone

gancho_primeira_frase: my parents continued to offer my medical services for free to family members so they
texto_falado_limpo_en: my parents continued to offer my medical services for free to family members so they received diagnoses that traumatized the entire family i am 23 years old and in my second year of physician assistant school i am not a doctor but i am trained to diagnose reset and assist in surgeries under supervision three nights a week i work in the er during my rotations my parents decided that made me the family s free health service at first i helped aunt susan s rash cousin derek s mole uncle dave s chest pain family help family but the situation escalated in a way that i did not anticipate first my mom started giving my number to people i barely knew i would receive messages at 11 at night with photos of hives and questions about drug interactions i explained that i cannot diagnose without tests that doing so could cost me my license before i have it she told me i was being difficult it takes you 2 seconds to respond then he started promising my time without telling me i would arrive at family dinners and find someone waiting for me with a handwritten list of symptoms i told him straight you need to ask me before you offer i have school rotations real patients she became defensive you pay 800 a month in rent it s half the market price don t be selfish the third episode was the one that made me decide last month he volunteered me to do medication reviews for 12 seniors in his church 4 hours on a sunday i found out the night before through a text message tomorrow at 2 don t be late i responded that i had a pharmacology exam that was worth 30 of my grade his response was these people are counting on you that s when i stopped arguing thanksgiving was at our house 30 people the day before mom reminded me with all the naturalness in the world aunt susan wants me to see her ankle uncle dave has questions about his medications i smiled sure mom dinner was noisy plates full everyone relaxed i waited for the exact moment just before dessert when no one is in a hurry to leave i stood up some people asked for medical opinions i m going to take advantage of the fact that we re all here i took out my phone as if i had notes aunt susan the constant thirst you mentioned along with that wound on your foot that hasn t healed in three weeks could be clear signs of undiagnosed type 2 diabetes you need a glucose panel this week uncle dave snoring like this every night with those episodes where you stop breathing is severe sleep apnea without treatment heart risk doubles in 5 years nobody spoke dad the pressure you marked at the pharmacy last month 158 out of 100 sustained like this may be causing silent kidney damage that is not ignored mom that nodule on your neck that you say you haven t checked for years has grown you need an ultrasound as soon as possible the forks were suspended in the air cousin karen you have had extreme fatigue for months and bruises that appear on their own that may indicate severe anemia or something more serious in the blood grandma the slight tremor in your right hand that you ve been hiding under the table since i arrived early parkinson s starts exactly like this total silence my mom grabbed my wrist from under the table what s the matter are you scaring everyone i looked at her i m doing what you asked me to do aunt susan was shaking uncle dave already had the phone in his hand my dad touched his neck slowly as if he had just discovered it was there you just told your father that he has kidney damage mom hiss i have no idea if he has kidney damage i ve never looked into it properly i took my fork that is why medicine is not practiced at thanksgiving dinner my dad spoke quietly so you made it up for me the symptoms are real diagnoses are possibilities that uncertainty is exactly why you go to a doctor in private with time i sat down and cut a piece of cake they left early quiet two days later my mother came into my room you can t do that again so ask me first he texto_falado_limpo_en: i raised my husband s son as my own for 5 years then a dna test meant for a school project revealed something that made my husband pack a bag and leave but not for the reason you think when i married derek his son mason was 2 years old his biological mother left when mason was 6 months old just vanished one morning with a note that said i m not built for this dererick raised that boy alone for a year and a half before i came along i loved mason the second i met him i didn t just step into the role of his mother i became her every school event every doctor s appointment every nightmare at 3 00 a m i taught him to read i taught him to tie his shoes i held him when kids at school asked why he didn t look like his mom and i told him families don t have to match to belong to each other i legally adopted mason when he was four dererick cried that day he held my face and said you chose us nobody ever chooses us his parents treated me like gold his mother would say that boy has no idea how lucky he is to have you i gave up my chance to have my own biological children because dererick said he didn t want more kids and i told myself mason was enough he was more than enough he was everything when mason was seven his school assigned a genetics project compare traits with your parents eye color earlobes tongue rolling simple stuff mason came home confused because nothing matched i explained gently that i wasn t his biological mom but that it didn t change how much i loved him he hugged me and said you re my real mom i don t care about the science that should have been the end of it but dererick got quiet that night really quiet music the next morning i found a receipt on the counter for a home dna test kit not for me and mason but for derrick and mason 3 days later the results came back derrick opened the envelope in the kitchen while i was folding laundry i heard him go completely silent then a chair scrape across the floor i walked in and he was sitting at the table with both hands flat on the surface like he was trying to stop the room from spinning i said what s wrong he slid the paper across the table 0 match mason was not dererick s biological son i said that s impossible there must be an error dererick shook his head i ordered two kits sent the second to a different lab this is right i said so his mother he cut me off she didn t just leave she left because she knew he wasn t mine and she didn t want to be around when i found out i watched dererick s entire identity as a father collapse in 90 seconds he kept saying i raised him i was there for every moment and none of it was real i grabbed his arm all of it was real you are his father a piece of paper doesn t erase seven years he pulled away you don t understand everything i believed about my life is a lie he packed a bag that night not because he stopped loving mason but because he couldn t look at him without seeing her betrayal i stood in mason s doorway that night and watched him sleep a boy already abandoned by one parent about to be abandoned by another i called dererick s mother the next morning and told her everything she was quiet for a long time and then she said two words we knew i said what do you mean you knew she said we suspected from the beginning mason didn t look like anyone in our family we had a test done when he was a baby privately without telling derrick the floor tilted under me i said you ve known for seven years that mason wasn t his and you never told him she said he was so happy we couldn t take that from him i said you let your son build his life on a lie you watched me give up having my own children for a family you knew was built on something fake and you said nothing she started crying i hung up dererick came back after 3 weeks not for me not for his parents for mason he walked through the door and mason ran to him screaming daddy where did you go dererick dropped to his knees and held that boy so tight i thought he d never let go he looked up at me and said he s my son i don t care what any test says we re still together his parents and i don t speak mason doesn t know one day we ll tell him but right now he s happy and he has two parents who chose him

gancho_primeira_frase: when did you realize that strict parents make the best liars my parents had an
texto_falado_limpo_en: when did you realize that strict parents make the best liars my parents had an ironclad rule when it came to dating absolutely forbidden until after high school otherwise they would send me to my grandmother in the country in mecklenburg vorpomann where the nearest boy my age lived for what felt like forever they had threatened to do this twice before after they caught me texting with boys at school after they took away my phone for a week and made me write a five page essay about why teenage romance destroys academic success i was completely distraught the problem was i was hopelessly in love with lukas the smartest boy in my history class and i was running out of time because he was changing schools at the end of the semester so i analyzed the wording of her rule very carefully they specifically said no dating and no boyfriend but they didn t mention anything about study partnerships or tutoring that became my loophole in the system i went to my parents with a suggestion for a learning program i would help weaker students with german and in return get support in subjects like math and history i emphasized how much this would improve my chances at university and show my initiative what i didn t mention was that luke would be my main study partner my parents were thrilled and suddenly allowed me to go outside longer after school to stay until 8 p m expressly for learning for the next month lukas and i met three times a week in the library in cafés and finally at our house when no one was there we really learned but we also kissed constantly and talked about everything for hours just not above school when my parents asked about progress i showed them my better math grades and luke s improved essays the system was working perfectly until something completely unexpected happened our fake learning program actually made us absolute high flyers lukas who previously had problems with writing suddenly started writing essays that delighted our teachers and i noticed that by explaining material myself i understood everything much better within six weeks we had improved our grades so much that other students were keen to join in then came the twist that changed everything luka s parents who were getting divorced decided that he had to move to munich immediately not just at the end of the semester he only had three days left i was devastated but i also realized something scary because of our study sessions together i was suddenly the best in my entire year without lukas i would be alone again and maybe average again then the school counselor called my parents and told them that their daughter was suddenly the favorite to win the title best of the year and then she made a fatal mistake she enthusiastically praised our romantic learning partnership and mentioned how our relationship had led to these incredible results when my parents heard the word romantic everything escalated that same evening they stormed into my room with suitcases pack your things my father said you re going to grandma s today so you ve had a boyfriend the whole time and you ve been lying to us my mother shouted you manipulated us into helping you break our most important rule i panicked and blurted out wait you can t send me away now i m about to be valedictorian i showed them my gpa my improved test scores and my first acceptances from universities the study thing really worked i said desperately i m ranked first out of over 300 students i received acceptances from all the universities i applied to and several even offered me a scholarship my father stared at me in disbelief you managed to become the best student in the school anyway i said quietly the relationship is over but i m still the best because of what we ve built help you become the most successful student in school they said the punishment but i was grounded until i graduated from high school except for school and the learning program that i now really had to run because twelve other students had signed up in the meantime four months later i gave the valedictorian speech as valedictorian lukas and i are still in touch who knows what the future will bring

gancho_primeira_frase: the doctor said they were perfectly compatible but only one could survive i m currently
texto_falado_limpo_en: the doctor said they were perfectly compatible but only one could survive i m currently cooking my daughter lena s favorite spaghetti exactly what she always wants after soccer training when my cell phone rings i see my husband jonas face on the screen but when i answer it i don t hear his voice ms schneider this is the st marinken hospital her husband and daughter were in a car accident please come immediately i don t remember how i got to the hospital suddenly i m just there run through automatic doors that open way too slowly the emergency room is full of people but i don t see any kids in soccer jerseys i don t see jonas gray jacket i go to reception is there a lena or jonas schneider here a nurse leads me down a hallway that smells like the infectious agent a doctor is waiting in front of two rooms his clothes are smeared with blood ms schneider i am dr weber please sit down but i can t sit i see jonas through a window machines breathe for him through the other lena so small under the white sheets a truck ran a red light he says he hit the driver s side i try to listen but the words blur both are in critical condition jonah s kidney is failing lena needs part of a liver both organs fail quickly i hold on to the wall because everything is spinning we can save them you can save her dr weber takes my hands his are quiet mine are shaking ms schneider you have to understand something her husband and daughter are perfect lovers for each other i feel dizzy what he says it again more slowly jonas could donate part of his liver to lena or lena s kidney could save jonas but only one of the two can be the donor the word hits me like a blow schwenders are people who die you want me to decide my voice sounds strange there are no other matching donors at the moment we may have another 24 hours he doesn t finish the sentence but i know what it means 24 hours before i lose them both and if someone donates they die he nods in her condition the donor would not survive the procedure i look back and forth between both rooms jonah s chest rises and falls with the machine lena s soccer jersey is folded on a chair number 7 your lucky number how should i choose between my husband to whom i have been married for 10 years and my eight year old daughter i sit between the two rooms six hours pass or at least that s what the clock says jonas opens his eyes first he can t speak but he squeezes my hand three times i love you then he falls asleep again an hour later lena wakes up mom she whispers where is dad i say he s sleeping next door she tries to put on my stomach hurts i brush her hair out of her face like i always do when she has bad dreams but this time it s not a dream my mother comes her eyes red from wine you have to save lena she says immediately she s still a child after all shortly afterwards jonah s brother arrives jonas was always there for you don t let him die they fight i just want you to stop the social worker keeps coming with the forms ms schneider we need a decision soon her voice is soft her words are not there is no wrong decision yes every decision is wrong last weekend jonas taught lena how to ride a bike she drew him a map best dad in the world it s still hanging on the fridge they were on their way home from training he was probably telling one of his bad jokes when the accident happened i go back to the counter there are two forms there one thing jonas donates his liver to lena the other lena donates her kidney to jonas no matter which one i sign i ll lose one of them i take the pen i want to sign then the door to the intensive care unit opens dr weber comes running in doctors don t run unless it s a matter of life and death wait he pulls the papers out from under my hand there is a donor in hamburg he says out of breath i stare at him a donor can mean everything wrong blood type too far away only one organ 25 years old motorcycle accident declared brain dead 20 minutes ago is he i can t finish the sentence it fits he says quietly my heart is pounding for whom is he looking at me for both i feel dizzy the family agreed the helicopter is already on the way in three hours we can perform both operations at the same time both he nods and smiles for the first time both a year later we planted a tree for the man whose death gave them life

gancho_primeira_frase: parents what s the craziest thing your child has ever said my daughter once went
texto_falado_limpo_en: parents what s the craziest thing your child has ever said my daughter once went to a complete stranger and told him that she really wanted to have a tree house before she went to heaven i had known for about a year that my little lena was dying she was six it was something i could barely say out loud the doctors explained everything to us in their own way a lot of technical language a lot of details but no matter how they put it it always meant the same thing with each passing week she became weaker with each passing day i felt like i was losing a piece of her that afternoon we just went to the hardware store to buy light bulbs and paint i turned around for maybe 30 seconds to grab something from a shelf when i looked again lena was gone i finally found them in the wood section she stood there and talked to a craftsman he was a big guy dusty jeans rough maybe mid 40s the kind of guy you immediately trust to build your terrace or repair your roof then i heard lena say clearly i want a tree house before i have to go to heaven direct honest just the way she always spoke the man froze at first he looked confused then his face softened he looked at me then back at lena and without hesitation he said okay then we ll build you one to be honest i thought it was a polite phrase as if you tell a sick child that he or she can grow up to be an astronaut nicely meant but without any real consequence but two days later the man showed up on our doorstep with sketches in his hand lena is completely freaked out with joy her eyes shone i hadn t seen her like this for months she grabbed the drawings pointed at everything asked a thousand questions and he answered every single one i began to look forward to his visits almost as much as she did he was calm reliable and incredibly friendly but i could tell something was bothering him as if this treehouse was also his own therapy sometimes he came by completely exhausted at some point i got worried one evening after dinner i went by his workshop and brought him coffee i found him sleeping on the floor the workshop was almost empty i woke him up carefully and he admitted that things were going badly in 30 days he would lose his workshop because he could no longer pay the rent he looked ashamed as he said this and stared at the floor then he said quietly i just want to get this ready for lena a few days later i overheard a phone call from him it sounded tense he had received a job offer good money safe work the catch he would have had to move away for six weeks too long lena might not have gotten her tree house in time he declined without hesitation and continued working then that came next message from the doctors lena had even less time than we thought maybe just a few weeks i drove home my hands numb on the steering wheel that evening lena asked very quietly dad will my tree house be finished on time it broke my heart i knew ah i had to do something i picked up my cell phone and started calling friends neighbors people from the hospital i told them about lena about the craftsman about the tree house and about how this man risked everything just to make her wish come true people listened a nurse posted our story online after lena told about it during a treatment then left everything very quickly messages calls help from everywhere and then the worst happened the next morning the craftsman was thrown out of his workshop completely devastated i can t finish building it i told him hold on help is on the way and sure enough the first transporters arrived in the afternoon first one then another and suddenly the whole street was full tradesmen construction workers neighbors even complete strangers they brought wood tools ladders everything we worked through the whole weekend lena sat snuggled up in blankets and watched everything she smiled bigger than she had in months cheering everyone on when it was finally finished they helped we climb up her she called it my heaven party everyone stood together laughed and rejoiced with her it was magical i stood under that treehouse tears in my eyes watching my little daughter get her last wish come true

gancho_primeira_frase: how did you cope with your extremely religious family when i was a child my
texto_falado_limpo_en: how did you cope with your extremely religious family when i was a child my family really forced our faith on us it wasn t something we just believed in it was something that determined our entire lives every single minute at 6 a m there was bible time before school prayers were said at every meal including before homework and in the evening there was a penitential circle in which we had to confess everything we had thought said or done that might have disappointed god we weren t even allowed to say omg harry potter was banned and even dinosaurs were not allowed to be mentioned my mom once burned my hoodie just because it had a skull on it she said it would attract demonic energy there was hardly any television just veggies or old grainy vhs tapes with sermons from the 80s music just praise or nothing at all one time i was humming a taylor swift song very quietly while cleaning my father made me copy psalm 11 a total of 150 times as a spiritual discipline there was no dating at all we were never allowed to be alone with the opposite sex i wasn t even allowed to write to boys from church without my mother checking our internet history was checked every day when we deleted something it was called secret sinning and then the whole family had to fast to achieve deliverance yes it was that extreme so i did learned to play a role i was the perfect christian daughter i prayed out loud in youth group and led worship nights like it was second nature but inside i m broken by it the rules didn t make sense the feeling of guilt was always there and i didn t feel god just pressure just fear everything started to change when i moved out for college for the first time i had room to breathe i met people who didn t judge professors who encouraged questions friends who didn t constantly worry about whether their clothes were addictive enough i took a course in religious studies and read books that would have gotten me kicked out of my home i have not become a radical atheist i just started thinking for myself and that was enough when i came home that first summer i was calmer and more cautious i wore jeans and had my hair cut short my mother said i looked like a different person i just said thank you the big bang came two years later on thanksgiving my aunt asked me to say the table prayer and i just didn t do it i gave it to someone else my mother just looked at me quietly after dinner she confronted me in the kitchen you ve changed she said what s up i told her the truth that i don t believe that anymore i m not even sure i ever really believed that i needed space to find my own way without constantly being afraid of hell she screamed not cried screamed as if i had told her someone had died she called me a broom and said the college had given me over to satan my father became completely outraged and shouted i m a disgrace to our family and i m letting demons speak through me he took my phone away and said i m cut off from everything until i find my way back my younger siblings stood in the hallway and watched everything my mother got olive oil and smeared it on the walls and my forehead while she prayed in tongues she told me to kneel i didn t do it my father said i was no longer welcome my little sister cried and asked if i was going to hell i said no and my mother screamed i would drag her to ruin with me i left that same night with only one bag no farewell just silent and slamming doors i spent the rest of the holidays alone in the dorm i tried to call a friend but i couldn t speak because i was crying i lay on this little bed and stared at the ceiling as i tried to remember the last time i felt like myself in this house they don t have me for christmas invited the following year now four years have passed they rarely write to me anymore and when they do it s usually just a bible verse or a link to a sermon about lost children or the end of the world my little brother blocked me on instagram my mother sent me an old photo of me from school and wrote that she was praying for this girl to come back

gancho_primeira_frase: what weird secret did your partner hide from you my boyfriend and i have this
texto_falado_limpo_en: what weird secret did your partner hide from you my boyfriend and i have this rule we each get one don t ask don t tell moment from our past something that happened before we met that we re allowed to just keep in the vault no judgment no excavation just respect the boundary and move on i used mine on day one told him i got fired from my first job for accidentally setting off a sprinkler system during a team meeting soaked the ceo ruined about 30 000 worth of equipment he laughed so hard he had to sit down said that was the most harmless dark secret he d ever heard and that he was relieved i wasn t hiding something actually weird then he told me his he s afraid of butterflies not like oh they re a little creepy afraid like full body recoil will leave a garden party afraid i watched this man see a monarch land on a fence post and physically flinch backward into a hedge i thought that was as vulnerable as we were going to get 2 years in i genuinely believed we had the kind of relationship where we knew everything that mattered there were no red flags no strange gaps in his stories nothing that didn t add up but every single saturday morning same exact rhythm he d get up at 6 45 a m kiss me on the forehead and say going for my run be back in an hour i m not a morning person i d roll over go back to sleep and wake up to him in the shower an hour later like clockwork 2 years every saturday i never even thought about it then one saturday i woke up early with a migraine couldn t fall back asleep decided to get up make coffee maybe catch him before he left i walked into the living room at 6 50 a m and froze he was standing in front of the tv in full running gear earbuds in following along to a zumba video not jogging in place not stretching full choreography hip swivels arm waves grape vine steps this man was committed to the routine i must have made a noise because he whipped around ripped the earbuds out and went completely pale his exact words how long have you been standing there long enough he tried to laugh it off at first said it was just a warm up but i didn t buy it the tv showed a playlist 15 videos deep all zumba all saved all labeled by week so he sat down and told me the whole thing in college he took a zumba class as a joke with his roommate thought it would be funny turns out he loved it actually got good at it but his friend found out and roasted him so hard he never went back he s been doing it in secret ever since alone at 6 45 a m every saturday for 7 years he said he thought if i ever found out i think he was ridiculous that i d tell people that it would become the thing i told him i didn t care that honestly i respected the discipline that if zumba made him happy he should own it he seemed to believe me we hugged it out he went back to his run i went back to bed now every single saturday morning when he kisses my forehead and says going for my run i lie there awake thinking about him grape vinding across the living room in our tiny apartment and i don t say a word because we made a deal also because last week i saw a butterfly near the trash cans and watched him sprint back inside so fast he left his keys in the

gancho_primeira_frase: when i was one year old a doctor made such a serious mistake during surgery
texto_falado_limpo_en: when i was one year old a doctor made such a serious mistake during surgery that my parents received millions in compensation now i m 18 and that money is gone when i was a baby i had surgery for a hernia it was not something complicated it was a risk free surgery to a certain extent but the surgeon made a serious mistake i m not going to go into the medical details because it s painful to remember but basically they caused permanent damage that has affected my entire life my parents sued the hospital obviously the case lasted for years but they finally settled out of court a significant sum i know that for as long as i can remember every time i asked about it my parents told me the same thing that money is saved for you for your future for your education i grew up with that certainty i imagined that when i turned 18 they would sit me down explain everything to me and we could plan together how to use it for college but when my birthday came around two months ago and i brought it up the response i received chilled my blood i told them that i wanted to talk about the compensation money that i was already of age and needed to start planning my college future my mom and dad looked at each other with a strange uncomfortable expression and my dad simply said it s too early to talk about that the time will come i froze too soon i m 18 i m legally an adult that money is mine because of what happened to me i insisted them i said i needed to at least know where it was invested in what account how much there was nothing just evasion trust us we are handling it well in due time i began to feel a knot in my stomach something didn t add up the next few days i began to notice things things that didn t seem strange to me before but now took on a different meaning about three years ago my parents bought a new house a big nice house in a much better neighborhood than where we lived before at that moment they told me that they had saved that my dad s business was going well i didn t question anything two years ago my dad opened his own workshop he used to work for someone else but suddenly he had his own place with new equipment several employees i was proud of him i thought he deserved it but now with all this money i started to wonder where the capital for all this came from one day i saw my mother taking photos of a trip they took to europe when i was about 15 years old i didn t go because i had school but they went away for two weeks i remember that at that moment i thought that they deserved it that they worked hard but now the question gnawed at me with what money did they pay for that trip i felt betrayed i felt used that money was mine it was compensation for something horrible that happened to me which marked my body and my life forever and every time i tried to talk about it they would change the conversation or make me feel like i was being ungrateful we have given you everything my mom said you ve never lacked anything and she was right i never lacked anything but that wasn t the point the point was that that money was specifically for me for my future and it seemed like they had used it to build their own i got to a point where i didn t know what to do how do you confront your own parents how do you tell them that you think they stole your money because that s what i felt that they had stolen it from me that they had taken something that legally belonged to me and they had used it for them i was about to consult with a lawyer really i searched the internet i read forums i tried to understand what my rights were finally a week ago everything exploded i told them that i was not going to continue waiting they used the compensation money they used it to buy the house where we live they used it to open the workshop but then my dad took out some papers and showed them to me it turns out that i am a partner in the business my name is on the documents my participation corresponds exactly to the amount of money that was invested from my compensation the workshop the house all of that is technically also mine and my dad is already scheduled to start paying me monthly dividends that i will use for university he explained to me that since i am an only child all of this will eventually be mine anyway forms but in the meantime the money is working generating more money instead of being stored and losing value i felt terrible he had judged them so harshly i had doubted them and it turns out that all this time they were protecting my future in a way that i didn t understand i asked them for forgiveness my mom cried

gancho_primeira_frase: i have rejected all of my husband s name proposals for our baby for six
texto_falado_limpo_en: i have rejected all of my husband s name proposals for our baby for six months straight and i would do it again without hesitation my husband mike and i found out we were having a boy in october and from that moment on his obsession with the name became a cold war within the house coming out of the ultrasound before we even took off our coats he pulled out his phone with a list he seemed to have prepared for years leo sebastian michael i said no to everyone not because they were ugly but because something about their insistence made me uncomfortable he looked confused almost offended but continued ralph robert cristian no no and no he put the phone on the table and asked me what names i liked i shrugged and told him he d know it when he heard it that i was looking for a connection he wasn t offering that became our pattern throughout the second quarter every night he suggested names every night i rejected them without hesitation he started getting creative literary heroes constellations characters from movies we both supposedly loved i dismissed everything with a coldness that was driving him to despair by december mike s frustration was evident one night in bed he turned to me with tired eyes and said he had counted 62 rejected names he sat up abruptly and accused me of wanting to erase him from our son s life before he was born i responded that i was just demanding that this was the most important decision of ours lives and that i wasn t going to settle for something mediocre he accepted the answer but something in his expression made it clear that he had broken the following week his sister clara came to dinner and the tension exploded she already knew about our discussions and with a tight smile she mentioned that mike had told her that the baby should be called thiago my fork stopped in mid air no i said firmly clara insisted that he was a beautiful man i repeated that it wasn t the right one and mike upset got up from the table without saying a word clara leaned toward me and quietly said that after years of knowing me she knew i was hiding something i started crying in front of the half finished dishes as she pulled her chair closer i took a deep breath and told him the truth that had been consuming me for months i told him about vanessa mike s ex girlfriend who couldn t have children and who since we announced the pregnancy had been sending him daily messages i told him how i wasn t just suggesting names but i was designing the baby s room via chat sending photos of cribs and colors that mike would then present to me as his ideas leo sebastian michael they were all off vanessa s list clara s face turned gray when i showed her the most disturbing message i can t wait for my little prince to wear the clothes i bought him mike was allowing his ex to live out a motherhood fantasy through me clara stood up so quickly that the chair hit the wall he shouted mike s name with a fury that i had never seen him when he returned confused she demanded his phone he tried to resist by talking about privacy but ended up giving in clara checked the messages for just a few seconds before stiffening he showed him the screen and demanded an explanation mike s face fell he said he just felt sorry for vanessa that she was depressed and he didn t think it was serious to let her participate a little i stood up feeling a clarity i hadn t had in months i yelled at her that every name every color and every detail came from her i confessed that i had known this for a long time and that i was just waiting to see how far his nerve would go in lying to me while i was carrying his son he stammered that it wasn t a real lie that he was just trying to keep peace with his past keep peace by letting your ex name our son clara took her bag told him that his parents had raised him better and left leaving us in a heavy silence mike tried to fix it for hours but the damage was already done i showed her the message where vanessa was already calling the baby by the name she had chosen as if it belonged to her that same night i packed his things and gave him a legal ultimatum i block vanessa yes but the trust no longer existed in the end i chose the name myself while signing the papers of our separation my son s name is arthur the name of the grandfather mike always hated now every time you hear it you ll have to remember that you lost to his family for trying to please a ghost

texto_falado_limpo_en: has anyone ever followed a family tradition that secretly ruined everything every christmas eve the wilson family followed one unbreakable tradition everyone had to share one honest truth before opening gifts no lies no filters it was supposed to bring them closer this year ethan felt nervous sitting by the fireplace in their cozy new york home he watched his family laugh pretending everything was perfect but he knew something they didn t when his turn came his mom smiled warmly your truth ethan he paused then said i ve been hiding something from all of you the room went silent his dad leaned forward serious now i lost my job 2 months ago shock spread instantly his mom looked hurt his sister whispered why didn t you tell us ethan just stared at the floor the mood was gone the tradition had done its job truth was out but later that night ethan checked his phone and smiled a message popped up congratulations you re promoted he hadn t lost his job he just wanted to see how his family would react in tough times they didn t comfort him they judged him first so tell me does truth really bring people closer or just expose them

gancho_primeira_frase: what s the funniest excuse you ve given a cop that completely backfired last saturday
texto_falado_limpo_en: what s the funniest excuse you ve given a cop that completely backfired last saturday i was driving to a concert with my boys marcus and dev i was doing 90 in a 60 zone because dev swore the exit was in half a mile and it was not blue lights my stomach drops officer reyes walks up flashlight in my face license and registration you were doing 90 in a 60 that s reckless driving you re looking at a 500 fine points on your license and i could impound this vehicle right now dev kicks my seat and whispers make something up or we re walking home so i panic and say the dumbest thing imaginable officer i m so sorry my little sister is having her quinceanera right now and i m supposed to give the toast i m the only brother she has and she ll never forgive me if i miss it i don t have a little sister officer reyes looks at me then looks at marcus and dev who are both nodding like their lives depend on it she narrows her eyes where s the venue my brain short circuits i blurted out the first place i can think of rosa s banquet hall on 5th street i drove past it 10 minutes ago and saw balloons outside she stares at me for what feels like a year then she says the five worst words of my life follow me i ll escort you she walks back to her patrol car hits the lights and pulls out marcus grabs my arm bro there s no quinceanera i know dev is already googling rosa s banquet hall there s actually an event there tonight we pull into the parking lot behind officer reyes and there are pink and white decorations everywhere music is blasting from inside there s a real quinceanera happening for a girl i have never met dev says i m going in before i can stop him he s sprinting inside i m standing in the parking lot with officer reyes and i can feel sweat rolling down my back two minutes later dev comes back with a man in a suit he walks right up to me shakes my hand and says loud enough for officer reyes to hear finally your sister has been asking for you all night get inside i have no idea who this man is he leans in and whispers your friend told me everything my daughter thinks this is the funniest thing she s ever heard she said if you don t come in and give a toast she s going to be genuinely disappointed officer reyes is smiling now she follows us to the door i walk into this banquet hall and there s like 200 people a dj a dance floor a girl in a huge pink dress spots me and screams he s here she runs over grabs my arm and whispers i m sofia you re my brother carlos you were stuck in traffic my dad told the whole family everyone s in on it don t mess this up the dj gets on the mic everyone sofia s big brother carlos finally made it the entire room erupts people are cheering an abuela walks up and pinches my cheeks someone hands me a plate of food and officer reyes is standing by the door looking genuinely emotional then sofia s dad taps a glass and says carlos we ve been waiting for you it s time for your toast the room goes quiet 200 people staring at me officer reyes is recording on her phone dev is in the corner dying i pick up a glass and i have no idea what i m about to say i look at sofia and she gives me this tiny nod like go ahead so i say sofia i know i m late i m always late but there is nowhere else i d rather be tonight than right here watching you become the incredible woman i always knew you d be the room explodes sofia s mom is crying her dad is crying marcus is crying and he doesn t even know these people sofia hugs me so hard i almost dropped my drink after the toast officer reyes comes up to me that was beautiful your sister is lucky to have you sofia jumps in officer thank you for bringing him here we were so worried officer reyes pulls out her ticket book and i freeze she tears out a page it s a warning next time leave earlier your sister shouldn t have to wait as we re driving away dev is laughing so hard he can barely breathe bro you just gave a quinceanera toast for a stranger sofia s dad texted dev that night he said it was the best toast of the night and sofia wants to invite me to her birthday next month i got a warning instead of a 500 ticket a plate of the best food i ve ever eaten and i m now honorary family to people i met 45 minutes ago dev still has the video of my toast he threatens to post it every time i won t drive him somewhere

gancho_primeira_frase: siblings of psychopaths when did you realize that your sibling wasn t normal when my
texto_falado_limpo_en: siblings of psychopaths when did you realize that your sibling wasn t normal when my little sister lena was 8 she asked me why you apologize and why people forgive you even if you don t mean it at all when i was 13 i had the feeling for the first time that something was wrong and i was right a few years later we were in the park together when she pushed a child off the swing he broke his arm and i had to give his mother 300 for the treatment i wanted to tell our mother the truth but when she came home from work she collapsed in the kitchen and started crying because she was completely exhausted she worked around 60 hours every week and the last thing i wanted to do was put more strain on her i kept it for myself but before i knew it protecting my mother from lena became my main responsibility i turned down two scholarships ended multiple relationships and essentially became a third parent just to keep my uncontrollable sister under control and worst of all i barely had a relationship with my mother anymore because every time lena did something i took the blame when she was 14 she took my mother s car for a spin and returned when it was barely drivable i claimed i caused the accident and paid almost 10 000 for repairs when she was 16 she started a fire in the garage when i did it could delete damage of around 120 000 had already been caused i told my mother i fell asleep with a space heater on but when she was 18 she did something that even i couldn t cover up anymore i don t know if it was boredom or if she just wanted to see how far she could go but one day she gambled away our mother s entire savings on an online gambling app she bet everything on red and lost this time i told her that i would tell our mother the truth but she was quicker when i got home she was already sitting at the kitchen table crying and telling a completely absurd story about how she had been afraid of me for years how i had forced her to keep quiet about all the terrible things she said i had done and my mother believed her she looked at me as if i were a stranger as if i hadn t raised her daughter for 23 years that was my turning point and i just went to my room and packed my things when my mother wasn t looking lena came in and kind of apologized she just said it was nothing personal and then asked if she could borrow 10 when i was ready to leave home forever i wanted to give my mother one last chance to listen to me but before i could say anything she just said i m disgusting and should go away it s been a month now and for the first time i have my own life in my own apartment i still worry about my mom but i know she will call me when she needs me and i will always be there for her

gancho_primeira_frase: what did you think was great but ended up being terrible growing up i thought
texto_falado_limpo_en: what did you think was great but ended up being terrible growing up i thought i had a superpower i could fall asleep at any time and stay asleep when other people talked about having trouble sleeping i had no idea what they were talking about how could sleeping be hard on girl scout trips i was always the only child who woke up feeling rested after a good night s sleep i was able to just lay on the floor fall asleep and sleep through the night my family made fun of me for falling asleep at school or watching boring movies but in reality they were just jealous of me because my mother has bad insomnia but what should i say sleep was just something natural for me when i was very little maybe i really thought i was a superhero sleep woman i told my friends about my magical ability to fall asleep at any time but they didn t believe me until i showed them i was able to just close my eyes while sitting and fall asleep in no time there was just one part of my superpower that wasn t great even though i could sleep at any time and obviously slept well every night i was always tired but i thought it was just because i knew how great sleep is of course my body wanted more of it why should i watch tv do homework or one listen to boring conversation when i could just sleep instead but when i was 17 everything changed i had just gotten my driver s license and was driving home after school when i suddenly became very tired i started dreaming about how good a nap would feel once i got home and could lie down on the couch and then suddenly i woke up the front of my car had crashed into the car in front of me my arms were bleeding and my whole body was throbbing with pain i was horrified i always knew i could fall asleep in seconds but i had always had it under control the worst thing was that there was a little girl in the car in front of me and she was seriously injured after the paramedics examined me i had to undergo a whole examination by doctors and police to find out why i had fallen asleep at the wheel they wanted to make sure i wasn t driving recklessly or drunk but i wasn t that s when the doctors found out what had been going on my whole life and it hit me like a blow i didn t have a superpower i had narcolepsy a previously undiagnosed illness i didn t get in trouble for the accident but i have to live with the fact that i m the reason a little girl is in a wheelchair for the rest of her life and of course my driver s license was immediately revoked it s not safe for me too driving because you never know when i ll fall asleep there are jobs i can t do because it would be too dangerous if someone had to rely on me to operate a machine or do anything at all at a certain time i always wanted to be a teacher but i can t risk falling asleep and leaving a class full of kids unattended this gift that i thought was so great ended up destroying my life and that of an innocent child
vocabulario_falado: asleep sleep fall superpower driving just great thought time doctors boring girl falling driver license

texto_falado_limpo_en: parents when did you know you were raising your child correctly my eight year old son s biggest dream has always been to adopt a puppy so for his birthday this year we took him to an animal shelter there my son discovered a pit bull named frank and immediately fell in love with him the thing about frank was that he had an incredibly sad expression on his face and it broke my heart to see him like that the zookeeper told us that he had been severely mistreated in the past and was very afraid of people my husband and i weren t sure if adopting a traumatized pit bull was a good idea but our son stood his ground and told us that he wouldn t leave here without frank so frank came home with us that same day at first he was very shy and afraid of us he even growled at my husband when he tried to approach him but the more treats we gave him the more we took him for walks and the more my son played with him the more frank began to thrive frank slept in our son s room at night and every morning our son started the day by taking him for a walk after a few months frank s sad expression completely disappeared and he was now a happy and loving pit bull he has become an integral part of our family and we wouldn t trade him for the world

texto_falado_limpo_en: my fiancé s pretentious stepmother insisted on planning our entire wedding without asking us anything and no one came not even us diane came into ryan s life when he was 16 years old her father married her two years after the divorce and since then she has been trying to be the protagonist of a story in which no one put her in for almost a decade ryan puts up with her i put up with her that was the dynamic everything changed when ryan proposed to me in december in front of his family at christmas dinner i said yes before he finished the question dian from across the table was already pulling out her phone the call came three days later it was 9 in the morning on a tuesday when dian called me to tell me that she had already spoken to the rose wood manner that they had availability for the first saturday in october and that it was perfect for 300 people 300 ryan and i together don t know 300 people i calmly told him that ryan and i hadn t talked about anything yet that it was only november that we needed time she let out a long sigh like a disappointed mother and said look if left to their own devices they re going to end up getting married in a walmart parking lot i know what i m doing ryan called his dad that same night robert listened paused and said give her the pleasure you know what she s like that was it that was the moment ryan and i made the decision for the next three weeks dian acted as if our wedding was her life project he hired corney his niece as a wedding planner for 3 500 she booked her friend barbara s photographer for 4 200 he
